category_title: High-achieving students with proactive study habits
scenario_title: Metacognitive reflection on study habits after an exam
minimal_context: The student is reviewing bar graphs of common study approaches by grade during a Biology Supplemental Instruction session to determine the causes of their difficulty on a recent exam.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the bar graphs of common study approaches by grade from the recent exam and discuss what might have caused any difficulties.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm looking at the graph, and I already do what the A students do. I study alone primarily and complete the online practice exams on Mastering Bio, but my exam result was still disappointing.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think about that?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I came to every class and made sure to take breaks while studying in a quiet environment. Seeing this anonymous data usually makes me feel more confident, but right now I just can't determine the causes of my difficulty.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try to think about other ways to study.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm just lacking a confidence level in the course right now. Honestly, I'm having reactionary beliefs about the whole thing and feel like I should just go back to employing shallow methods instead of trying to combine all these habits.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the bar graphs of common study approaches by grade from the recent exam and discuss what might have caused any difficulties.

STUDENT: I'm looking at the graph, and I already do what the A students do. I study alone primarily and complete the online practice exams on Mastering Bio, but my exam result was still disappointing.

TUTOR: What do you think about that?

STUDENT: I came to every class and made sure to take breaks while studying in a quiet environment. Seeing this anonymous data usually makes me feel more confident, but right now I just can't determine the causes of my difficulty.

TUTOR: Try to think about other ways to study.

STUDENT: I'm just lacking a confidence level in the course right now. Honestly, I'm having reactionary beliefs about the whole thing and feel like I should just go back to employing shallow methods instead of trying to combine all these habits.

task.content: Discussing and reflecting on study habit combinations during a group SI session after viewing peer data
task.learning_goal: determine the causes of my difficulty

context.materials: anonymous results / everyday data from surveys, bar graphs of common approaches by grade, online practice exams on Mastering Bio, study guides
context.social_setting: group SI session / peer fellow study sessions

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: After students receive disappointing exam results
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: lacking confidence level in the course
